concepts.finding: Recurrent recommender networks: user and item latent factors evolve over time via RNNs (LSTM/GRU). Each timestep, user and item factors from previous step updated based on new interactions. Temporal cross product: user_factor(t) * item_factor(t) predicts rating at time t. Outperforms static MF and time-biased MF by 10-20%.
concepts.implication: Temporal keyword embedding evolution: keyword embeddings updated over time as new co-occurrence patterns emerge. RNN captures smooth temporal drift (seasonal keywords, trending topics). Time-dependent embedding enables current-intent prediction.
concepts.open_question: How much do keyword embeddings drift over weeks/months? Is RNN-based temporal evolution necessary or does periodic retraining suffice?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: Recurrent keyword embeddings (RNN-driven temporal evolution) maintain higher intent classification accuracy during seasonal shifts than static embeddings retrained monthly.
